steps.description: Click the "Declarative Agent – Agents for Microsoft 365 Copilot" option in the "New Project" dropdown to start creating a new declarative agent within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface in Visual Studio Code.
Click the "No Action" entry in the dropdown menu under the "Add an action to your declarative agent" header, located within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface in Visual Studio Code.
Click the "Default folder" dropdown in the "Workspace Folder" dialog to set the project root folder path (`/home/.vscode/AgentsToolkitProjects`).

parameters.sample:
=== Generated Script ===
Language: bash

```bash
file_path="/home/vscode/AgentsToolkitProjects/vscuse_app_MXDYK/m365agents.yml"

if [ -f "$file_path" ]; then
  sed -i '/^provision:/,/^\S/ {/provision:/!d}' "$file_path"
  echo "Successfully removed all actions inside 'provision' while retaining the header."
else
  echo "Error: File does not exist at $file_path"
  exit 1
fi
```

=== Execution Results ===
Exit Code: 0

=== STDOUT ===
Successfully removed all actions inside 'provision' while retaining the header.

✓ Script executed successfully

steps.description: @code remove all actions inside provision, but don't remove "provision:" in file "/home/vscode/AgentsToolkitProjects/${{var:app_name}}/m365agents.yml"
Click on the file "m365agents.yml" in the Explorer view of Visual Studio Code.
Right-click on the "m365agents.yml" file tab in the Visual Studio Code editor's tab bar to access the context menu for file options.
Click the "Close" option in the context menu of the selected file tab ("m365agents.yml") within the Visual Studio Code application interface.
Press the F1 key to open the Command Palette in the Visual Studio Code interface, with the Explorer view currently active.
Type the text 'provision' into the command palette search input field in Visual Studio Code.
Press the "Enter" key to select the command "Microsoft 365 Agents: Provision" from the Command Palette in Visual Studio Code.
Type text: 'dev' into the input field of the "Select an environment" dropdown in the Visual Studio Code interface. This action triggers filtering or selection within the dropdown menu.
Press the "Enter" key to confirm the selected environment ("dev") in the "Select an environment" dropdown in Visual Studio Code.
@assertion '[ConfigManager.InvalidYamlSchemaError]: Unable to parse yaml file' is visible
Use keyboard shortcut: ctrl+` to toggle the integrated terminal in Visual Studio Code, interacting within the "VSCode" interface and context.

Use keyboard shortcut: `Ctrl + `` to focus on the integrated terminal in Visual Studio Code while working with the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit project, as shown in the interface with a YAML parsing error message displayed below.
Press the F1 key to open the command palette in the Visual Studio Code interface.

Type 'deploy' into the command palette input field in Visual Studio Code to trigger a command. The input field is located at the top of the interface and displays the prompt "Type the name of a command to run."
Press the "Enter" key to execute the "Microsoft 365 Agents: Deploy" command from the Visual Studio Code command palette.
Type text: 'dev' into the environment selection input field in the "Select an environment" dropdown within the Visual Studio Code application interface.
Press the "Enter" key to confirm the selection of the highlighted environment labeled "dev" in the "Select an environment" dropdown within the Visual Studio Code interface.
@assertion '[ConfigManager.InvalidYamlSchemaError]: Unable to parse yaml file' is visible

Use keyboard shortcut: ctrl+` to toggle the integrated terminal within the Microsoft Visual Studio Code interface, which is currently displaying the "Explorer" panel and an error message in the "OUTPUT" tab related to a YAML parsing issue.

Use keyboard shortcut: `Ctrl+`` to toggle the terminal pane in Visual Studio Code, currently showing an error dialog related to an invalid YAML schema file in the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension.
Press the F1 key to open the command palette in the Visual Studio Code interface.

Type text 'Microsoft 365 agents: publish' into the Command Palette input field in Visual Studio Code. The Command Palette is accessed via the top-center text box, with a suggestion list displayed below it. Ensure the first option ('Microsoft 365 agents: Publish') is highlighted before proceeding.
Press the "Enter" key to execute the selected command "Microsoft 365 Agents: Publish" in the Visual Studio Code Command Palette.
Type text: 'dev' into the input field of the "Select an environment" dropdown in Visual Studio Code.
Press the "Enter" key to confirm the selected environment ("dev") in the "Select an environment" dropdown within the Visual Studio Code interface.
@assertion '[ConfigManager.InvalidYamlSchemaError]: Unable to parse yaml file' is visible
